The internet's origins trace to 1960s Cold War research. In 1962, J.C.R. Licklider at MIT wrote memos describing a "Galactic Network" concept [1]. Internet history reportedly spans five decades. In the 1960s, ARPA allegedly commissioned ARPANET, implementing packet-switching concepts from Paul Baran and Donald Davies [1]. According to accounts, the first ARPANET message was sent October 29, 1969, between UCLA and Stanford [2].
